customUrl.description: Provide custom nocodb app base url

get.description: List all rows from **event** table. Fields to be included in the response can be refined through query parameters. Additionally, filtering, sorting, and pagination can be applied to the results.

parameters.name: fields
parameters.description:
Specify fields to include in the API response. 

Example: fields=`field1` will include only field1 in the response.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description:
Allows you to specify the fields by which you want to sort the records in your API response. Each sort object must have a 'field' property specifying the field name and a 'direction' property with value 'asc' or 'desc'. If **viewId** query parameter is also included, the sort included here will take precedence over any sorting configuration defined in the view. 

Example: sort=`{"direction":"asc", "field":"field1"}` will sort records in ascending order based on field1.

parameters.name: where
parameters.description:
Enables defining conditions to filter records in the API response. Multiple conditions can be combined using the logical operators 'and' or 'or'. Each condition consists of three components: a field name, a comparison operator, and a value.

Example: where=`(field1,eq,value1)~and(field2,eq,value2)` will filter records where field1 equals value1 AND field2 equals value2.

If **viewId** parameter is also included, these filters are applied on top of the view’s predefined filter configuration. 

**NOTE**: Maintain the specified format; do not include spaces between components of a condition. For further information on this please see [the documentation](https://nocodb.com/docs/product-docs/developer-resources/rest-apis#v3-where-query-parameter)

parameters.name: page
parameters.description:
Controls pagination of the API response by specifying the page number to retrieve. By default, the first page is returned. Increment the page number to retrieve subsequent pages.

Example: page=`2` will return the second page of data records in the dataset.

parameters.name: nestedPage
parameters.description:
Controls pagination of nested (linked) records in the API response by specifying the page number. By default, the first page is returned; increment the page number to retrieve subsequent pages.

Example: nestedPage=`2` will return the second page of nested data records in the dataset.

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description:
Sets a limit on the number of records returned in the API response. By default, all available records are returned, but this parameter allows you to control the quantity.

Example: pageSize=`100` will limit the response to 100 records per page.

Fetches records that are visible within a specific view. If the view has sorting enabled, the API returns records in the same order as displayed in the view. Specifying a **sort** query parameter overrides the view’s sorting configuration. Similarly, a **where** query parameter applies additional filtering on top of the view’s filters. By default, all fields—including those disabled in the view—are included in the response. Use the **fields** query parameter to include or exclude specific fields and customize the output.

**Views:**
* vwokamja42d972ql - Default view
parameters.name: viewId

nestedNext.description: Nested pagination token for next page

nestedPrev.description: Nested pagination token for previous page

post.description: Insert a new row in table by providing a key value pair object where key refers to the column alias. All the required fields should be included with payload excluding `autoincrement` and column with default value.

patch.description: Partial update row in table by providing a key value pair object where key refers to the column alias. You need to only include columns which you want to update.

delete.description: Delete a row by using the **primary key** column value.

parameters.name: recordId
parameters.description: Primary key of the record you want to read.

parameters.name: fields
parameters.description:
Specify fields to include in the API response. 

Example: fields=`field1` will include only field1 in the response.

get.description: Read a row data by using the **primary key** column value.

parameters.description:
Fetches records that are visible within a specific view. If the view has sorting enabled, the API returns records in the same order as displayed in the view. Specifying a **sort** query parameter overrides the view’s sorting configuration. Similarly, a **where** query parameter applies additional filtering on top of the view’s filters. By default, all fields—including those disabled in the view—are included in the response. Use the **fields** query parameter to include or exclude specific fields and customize the output.

**Views:**
* vwokamja42d972ql - Default view
parameters.name: viewId

get.description: Get rows count of a table by applying optional filters.

parameters.name: where
parameters.description:
Enables defining conditions to filter records in the API response. Multiple conditions can be combined using the logical operators 'and' or 'or'. Each condition consists of three components: a field name, a comparison operator, and a value.

Example: where=`(field1,eq,value1)~and(field2,eq,value2)` will filter records where field1 equals value1 AND field2 equals value2.

If **viewId** parameter is also included, these filters are applied on top of the view’s predefined filter configuration. 

**NOTE**: Maintain the specified format; do not include spaces between components of a condition. For further information on this please see [the documentation](https://nocodb.com/docs/product-docs/developer-resources/rest-apis#v3-where-query-parameter)

parameters.name: linkFieldId
parameters.description:
**Links Field Identifier** corresponding to the relation field **Links** or **LinkToAnotherRecord** established between tables.

Link Columns:
* cq4gdyqypdqqeed - panels
* cnfurne9c82gyst - registrations

parameters.name: recordId
parameters.description: Primary key of the record you want to read.

get.description: This API endpoint allows you to retrieve list of linked records for a specific `Link field` and `Record ID`. The response is an array of objects containing Primary Key and its corresponding display value.

parameters.name: fields
parameters.description:

nestedNext.description: Nested pagination token for next page

success.description: Indicates whether the linking operation was successful

requestBody.description: Record objects to be linked, each containing an id field

post.description: This API endpoint allows you to link records to a specific `Link field` and `Record ID`. The request payload is an array of record-ids from the adjacent table for linking purposes. Note that any existing links, if present, will be unaffected during this operation.

parameters.name: recordId
parameters.description: Primary key of the record you want to read.

success.description: Indicates whether the unlink operation was successful

requestBody.description: Record objects to be unlinked, each containing an id field

delete.description:
This API endpoint allows you to unlink records from a specific `Link field` and `Record ID`. The request payload is an array of record-ids from the adjacent table for unlinking purposes. Note that, 
- duplicated record-ids will be ignored.
- non-existent record-ids will be ignored.

parameters.name: recordId
parameters.description: Primary key of the record you want to read.

**NOTE**: Maintain the specified format; do not include spaces between components of a condition. For further information on this please see [the documentation](https://nocodb.com/docs/product-docs/developer-resources/rest-apis#v3-where-query-parameter)

parameters.name: linkFieldId
parameters.description:
**Links Field Identifier** corresponding to the relation field **Links** or **LinkToAnotherRecord** established between tables.

Link Columns:
* c6elf5yvj50hudi - registrations

parameters.name: recordId
parameters.description: Primary key of the record you want to read.

get.description: This API endpoint allows you to retrieve list of linked records for a specific `Link field` and `Record ID`. The response is an array of objects containing Primary Key and its corresponding display value.

parameters.name: fields
parameters.description:
Specify fields to include in the API response. 

Example: fields=`field1` will include only field1 in the response.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description:
Allows you to specify the fields by which you want to sort the records in your API response. Each sort object must have a 'field' property specifying the field name and a 'direction' property with value 'asc' or 'desc'. If **viewId** query parameter is also included, the sort included here will take precedence over any sorting configuration defined in the view. 

Example: sort=`{"direction":"asc", "field":"field1"}` will sort records in ascending order based on field1.

parameters.name: where
parameters.description:
Enables defining conditions to filter records in the API response. Multiple conditions can be combined using the logical operators 'and' or 'or'. Each condition consists of three components: a field name, a comparison operator, and a value.

Example: where=`(field1,eq,value1)~and(field2,eq,value2)` will filter records where field1 equals value1 AND field2 equals value2.

If **viewId** parameter is also included, these filters are applied on top of the view’s predefined filter configuration. 

**NOTE**: Maintain the specified format; do not include spaces between components of a condition. For further information on this please see [the documentation](https://nocodb.com/docs/product-docs/developer-resources/rest-apis#v3-where-query-parameter)

parameters.name: page
parameters.description:
Controls pagination of the API response by specifying the page number to retrieve. By default, the first page is returned. Increment the page number to retrieve subsequent pages.

Example: page=`2` will return the second page of data records in the dataset.

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description:
Sets a limit on the number of records returned in the API response. By default, all available records are returned, but this parameter allows you to control the quantity.

Example: pageSize=`100` will limit the response to 100 records per page.

nestedNext.description: Nested pagination token for next page

nestedPrev.description: Nested pagination token for previous page

success.description: Indicates whether the linking operation was successful

requestBody.description: Record objects to be linked, each containing an id field

post.description: This API endpoint allows you to link records to a specific `Link field` and `Record ID`. The request payload is an array of record-ids from the adjacent table for linking purposes. Note that any existing links, if present, will be unaffected during this operation.

parameters.name: recordId
parameters.description: Primary key of the record you want to read.

success.description: Indicates whether the unlink operation was successful

requestBody.description: Record objects to be unlinked, each containing an id field

delete.description:
This API endpoint allows you to unlink records from a specific `Link field` and `Record ID`. The request payload is an array of record-ids from the adjacent table for unlinking purposes. Note that, 
- duplicated record-ids will be ignored.
- non-existent record-ids will be ignored.

parameters.name: recordId
parameters.description: Primary key of the record you want to read.

bearerAuth.name: Authorization
bearerAuth.description: Bearer token authentication. Use 'Authorization: Bearer <token>' header format. This is an alternative to the xc-token header.
